mnemonics: VOW: ValueOf Wrapper - Converts strings to wrapper objects like Integer, Boolean, or Double.
keyPoints: Converts a string to the corresponding wrapper class object (e.g., Integer.valueOf('123') returns an Integer)
Returns a cached object for certain values (e.g., Integer values between -128 and 127) for efficiency
Throws a NumberFormatException if the string cannot be parsed
commonMistakes: Confusing valueOf() with parseInt() - valueOf returns an object, parseInt returns a primitive
Forgetting it can throw a NumberFormatException and not handling it, causing crashes
Assuming the returned object is a new instance every time, not realizing it uses a cache for certain values
